response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with a strong web-focused career, but the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ for industrial automation — a domain where his exposure is marginal. His only substantial C++ work is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; his 17-year career is otherwise rooted in TypeScript, Node.js, and D. There is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL), MQTT or industrial protocols, multi-threaded C++ systems at scale, or people-management experience, all of which are central or required for this Senior Manager role.
strengths: Solid event-driven and high-performance distributed systems background (low-latency micro-services, async I/O at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Strong agile craftsmanship discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring, and public technical workshops demonstrate high engineering maturity
Demonstrated REST API design and IoT-adjacent platform work (GISCollective geo-data platform with event-driven architecture deployable on Kubernetes)
concerns: C++ proficiency is marginal — only a six-month role at NI and a bachelor thesis project; no evidence of production STL-heavy, multi-threaded, or embedded C++ systems
No trace of industrial cybersecurity knowledge: IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT)
No management or formal team-leadership experience anywhere on the resume despite the Senior Manager seniority level of the role

key_factors.factor: C++ depth and embedded systems experience
